page: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyperthreading, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at a base speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled directly, consisting of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth, managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25% tier. The processor's support for AVX-512 vector instructions and SHA cryptography extensions contributes to strong results in PassMark encryption and extended instruction tests. Software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks also rank in the top 25%. Memory performance is moderate, with read-only large block bandwidth at 165.54 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 66.11 GB/sec, both falling in the middle 50% tier.

The c6id.16xlarge offers a balanced profile for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local storage. The inclusion of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD storage alongside 64 vCPUs provides high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for applications that would otherwise require separate high-performance storage attachments. Strong database operations and static web serving throughput benchmarks indicate that this instance is well-suited for high-throughput database hosting, large-scale software compilation, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processors on the x86_64 architecture. It includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, software compilation, and database operations. While single-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, the combination of high-frequency compute and fast local storage offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive applications. This server is highly suited for database hosting, software compilation, web serving, and medium-scale machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A compute-optimized platform combining sixty-four high-frequency Intel vCPUs with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz
128 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU, software compilation, and database performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for high-throughput databases, web serving, and compilation tasks
